Authored_text:

"I'm pretty certain most, if not all, of those only do so because they date back to when setuptools tried to be the whole workflow instead of just a wheel builder and `python setup.py test` and the like were the norm.\n\nDesigning these tools to latch onto setuptools allowed them to *borrow* configuration like project name and version but the move to putting core metadata in the backend agnostic pyproject.toml's `[project]` section ([pep 621](https://peps.python.org/pep-0621/)) superseded that. If you look at [cxfreeze's usage guide](https://cx-freeze.readthedocs.io/en/stable/setup_script.html) you can see that, whilst the old `setup.py` based way is still there, the default has completely moved on; it's fully decoupled itself from setuptools using the shared `[project]` configuration for common metadata, its own configuration for the rest and its own standalone `cxfreeze build` command."

We need phrase candidates: maybe "I'm pretty certain", "only do so because they date back to when", "fully decoupled itself from setuptools". Let's check length: "I'm pretty certain" is 18 chars. "only do so because they date back to when" is 43 chars. "fully decoupled itself from setuptools" is 38 chars. All fine.

We need to preserve source characters exactly. So we must copy exactly as in text. Let's copy:

"I'm pretty certain" (exact). "only do so because they date back to when" (exact). "fully decoupled itself from setuptools" (exact). Ensure no trailing spaces.

Let's craft: "I think most of those tools still rely on setuptools because they originated when setuptools handled the whole workflow, but the shift to pyproject.toml's [project] section has made tools like cxFreeze fully independent from setuptools." That's 35 words. Good.
